mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate discount rates and borrowing costs, directly increasing the present value of future equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals rapid labor market deterioration and imminent recession, triggering immediate equity portfolio de-risking and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate collapsing consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative equity returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline MoM
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of co-declining months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals resilient supply-side capacity that offsets rate hike headwinds, generating positive equity returns as earnings growth outpaces discount rate increases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 3.5 percent, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity valuations, producing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in >55% of high-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: Steady monthly payroll growth above 150,000 sustains wage-driven consumption, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and yielding positive equity returns as earnings momentum accelerates.
failure_condition: PAYEMS above 150,000 correlates with negative 20-day returns in >50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where GDP growth decelerates while retail sales accelerate indicates inventory drawdowns and impending restocking cycles, which boost equity valuations over a quarter as corporate margins expand.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in industrial production and housing starts below their 12-month moving averages signals a broad-based demand shock that forces equity repricing downward over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both fall below 12-month MA
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >55% of instances when both series fall below their moving averages.
